author: lOrm
content: Fixed: McpServer::new now wraps the initialize handshake (initialize + tools/list) in tokio::time::timeout with a 30s default deadline. McpError::Timeout converted to a struct variant { phase: &'static str } so the timeout source ('initialize', 'tools/list', 'tools/call', 'http-request') is explicit. New public McpServerConfig (distinct from plugins::manifest::McpServerConfig which describes on-disk launch params) carries initialize_timeout_secs; 0 disables the deadline for callers that supply their own outer cancellation scope. McpServer::new uses default config; McpServer::new_with_config takes explicit config. 4 new tests via in-memory FakeTransport: timeout fires on stalled server (Timeout { phase: 'initialize' } in <5s vs 60s stall, 1s deadline), normal handshake succeeds, timeout is configurable (default=30s + 1s override honoured at runtime), zero disables deadline. All 1402 lib tests pass, cargo clippy --lib --tests -D warnings exits 0, no #[allow], no #[ignore], no clippy.toml changes.
